mechanism_narrative: The level of retail and food services sales (RSAFS) directly reflects consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings. Higher RSAFS leads to higher S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days because consumption is the largest component of US GDP.
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS level and forward 20-day return is not significantly positive (p>0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 4.0%, the labor market becomes excessively tight, forcing the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates to curb wage inflation. Higher interest rates depress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances when UNRATE < 4.0%, the median forward 60-day return is not negative (one-sided t-test p>0.05) or the signal fails to outperform a no-trigger baseline by 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is below 3.0% and retail sales (RSAFS) are strong, monetary policy is accommodative and consumer demand is robust, creating a favorable environment for corporate profits and equity returns. The joint signal is more powerful than either alone.
failure_condition: The conditional forward return when both conditions hold is not significantly higher than the unconditional mean (t-test p>0.05, one-tailed) over any 2-year period, or the interaction term in a linear regression is not significant at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, characterized by rising federal funds rates, the market anticipates slower economic growth and higher discount rates. This depresses equity prices, leading to negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rate increase from previous month
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month, the average forward 20-day return is not negative (one-sided test p>0.05) or the signal's information coefficient is below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures the real output of manufacturing, mining, and utilities. Sustained growth in INDPRO signals economic expansion and rising corporate earnings, supporting higher equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO level and forward 60-day return is not positive (rho<=0) over any 5-year period, or the predictive slope is not significantly different from zero (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When both nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) increase month-over-month, the economy is in a broad-based expansion. This dual confirmation reduces uncertainty about future earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both PAYEMS and INDPRO increase MoM
failure_condition: The average forward 20-day return in months when both conditions are met is not significantly positive (p>0.05 one-tailed) or it does not exceed the average return in months when at least one condition fails by at least 100 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1,200 thousand units indicate strong residential investment and consumer confidence. This robust demand feeds into broader economic growth, providing a short-term positive impulse to equity markets within one week.
failure_condition: The median forward 5-day return when HOUST > 1200 is not greater than zero (Wilcoxon signed-rank test p>0.05) or the win rate does not exceed 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 2.5%, the Federal Reserve is likely to tighten monetary policy to control inflation. Higher interest rates reduce the present value of future corporate earnings, leading to a negative outlook for the S&P 500 over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5%, the forward 60-day return is not significantly negative (p>0.05 one-tailed) or the signal fails to generate a Rank IC below -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
